DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT, IN VIOLATION OF LOCAL RULE 2.6B. A COURTESY COPY SHALL BE LODGED WITH THE COURT BY APRIL 13, 2015, OR THE MOTION WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED
MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-14-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CITY HOUSING, INC.AND TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED BASED ON CCP 430.10(F) AS TO THE 1ST (NEGLIGENCE) AND 8TH (BREACH OF CONTRACT) CAUSES OF ACTION FOR P TO RE-ALLEGE HIS CLAIMS CONTAINED IN THEREIN AS SEPARATE CAUSES OF ACTION. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING THIS CAUSE OF ACTION NOT BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 4TH - 7TH AND 9TH CAUSES OF ACTION. STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS HAS RUN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM, IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B), AND INCLUDING THE DISMISSAL IN THE ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS'' DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 8, 2015 ON COURT''S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, OURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS RULES AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. PAGES 16-30 OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION ARE STRICKEN. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE, NUISANCE AND VIOLATION OF STATUTORY WARRANTY AS THEY ARE NOT ADDRESSED IN PLAINTIFF'S 30-PAGE LONG OPPOSITION. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH A FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR IMPLIED WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY, TORTIOUS BREACH OF WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY AND BREACH OF COVENANT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING THESE CAUSES OF ACTION NOT BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. DEMURRER IS OTHERWISE OVERRULED. IT DOES NOT APPEAR ON THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT THAT THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION FOR EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS BARRED BY STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS, I.E. NO SPECIFIC MOVE-OUT DATE IS ALLEGED IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ADDITIONALLY, IN SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON THE GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF A BROAD LEAVE TO AMEND TO SEPARATE OUT ALL THE VARIOUS CAUSES OF ACTION ASSERTED TOGETHER UNDER THE HEADINGS OF NEGLIGENCE AND BREACH OF CONTRACT. NEWLY ASSERTED CAUSES OF ACTION (2, 3, 5, 6 AND 7) APPEAR TO BE BASED ON THE FACTS ALLEGED UNDER THOSE HEADINGS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-21-2015 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Jan-06-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-10-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
D.P. Ochoa (415-513-6130) Plaintiff/Moving party on his own behalf.
Beth O. Arnese Esq., (415-465-8888 ext 223) of Bradley, Curley, Asiano, Barrabee, Abel & Kowalski for Defendant/Opposing party City Housing, Inc.

Court having reviewed the pleadings rules as follows: Sustained in part and Overruled in part. Pages 16-30 of Plaintiff's opposition are stricken.
Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action for negligence, nuisance and violation of statutory warranty as they are not addressed in Plaintiff's 30-page long opposition. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the specific performance. Demurrer is sustained with a final leave to amend as to the causes of action for implied warranty of habitability, tortious breach of warranty of habitability and breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting these causes of action not barred by statute of limitations.

Demurrer is otherwise Overruled. It does not appear on the face of the complaint that the cause of action for Intentional Infliction for Emotional Distress is barred by statute of limitations, i.e. no specific move-out date is alleged in the Second Amended Complaint. Additionally, in sustaining the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint on the grounds of uncertainty the Court granted Plaintiff a broad leave to amend to separate out all the various causes of action asserted together under the headings of negligence and breach of contract. Newly asserted causes of action (2, 3, 5, 6 and 7) appear to be based on the facts alleged under those headings. Order is signed in open court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Continued To Sep-30-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2015 Continued To Sep-08-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants'' Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint is Continued to September 8, 2015 on Court''s own motion. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-10-2015 Continued To Aug-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-14-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Line 5
Appearances:
Daniel Ochoa, (415-255-9376) Plaintiff/ opposing party in Pro Per.
Beth Arnes, Esquire. of BCABA & K (415-464-888 x223)) for Defendant/ Moving party City Housing Inc., and Tenderloin Housing Clinic..

The Court informs the parties on an error on the tentative ruling. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 4th-7th and 9th causes of action. Statute of limitations has run. Defendant requests to dismiss city housing Inc. because of the merger with Tenderloin Housing clinic. Plaintiff does not oppose the request. The Court grants the request. Parties do not oppose the tentative ruling. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with modifications.

DEFENDANT CITY HOUSING, INC.and TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurrer is SUSTAINED based on CCP 430.10(f) as to the 1st (negligence) and 8th (breach of contract) causes of action for P to re-allege his claims contained in therein as separate causes of action. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to the 3rd cause of action with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting this cause of action not barred by statute of limitations. Demurrer is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the 4th - 7th and 9th causes of action. Statute of limitations has run. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form, in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b) and including the dismissal in the order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
